name: skill-supabase-rls
description: Defensive Row Level Security guidance for Supabase SaaS projects.
instructions: Use as a defensive guide for Supabase RLS design, validation, tenant isolation, service-role boundaries, and migration review.

orientation.purpose: Design and verify safe Supabase RLS policies.
orientation.authorization: Do not run production migrations or destructive SQL without explicit user approval.
orientation.adaptation: Adapt examples to the project's schema, roles, memberships, and storage model.

prerequisites: Supabase JavaScript client v2 or equivalent

recommendations.description: Enable RLS on user-facing tables in exposed schemas.
recommendations.rationale: RLS protects data even when tables are accessed through browser-safe API keys.

recommendations.description: Keep service-role keys out of frontend code.
recommendations.rationale: Service-role keys bypass RLS and can expose all tenant data if leaked.
Explicit roles make anonymous and authenticated access reviewable.

recommendations.description: Use USING for visibility and WITH CHECK for writes.
recommendations.rationale: Write checks prevent users from inserting or updating rows into another user's or tenant's scope.

recommendations.description: Validate tenant membership for tenant-scoped rows.
recommendations.rationale: Tenant predicates prevent cross-tenant reads and writes.
Indexes reduce latency and avoid policy-driven table scans.

recommendations.description: Test positive and negative access cases.
recommendations.rationale: A policy is not validated until both allowed and denied paths are proven.
